test.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel" && cargo test --manifest-path wrapper/Cargo.toml && npm --prefix shared run test && npm --prefix server run test && npm --prefix bot run test

typecheck.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel" && npx tsc --noEmit -p shared/tsconfig.json && npx tsc --noEmit -p server/tsconfig.json && npx tsc --noEmit -p bot/tsconfig.json

lint.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel" && npx eslint "server/src/**/*.ts" "bot/src/**/*.ts" && tmpdir=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$tmpdir" && apt-get download rust-clippy >/dev/null && dpkg-deb -x rust-clippy_*.deb "$tmpdir/extract" && PATH="$tmpdir/extract/usr/bin:$PATH" cargo clippy --manifest-path "/data/dl/fschannel/wrapper/Cargo.toml" --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

blockingIssues.description: shared/src/protocol.ts misclassifies non-tool item/started and item/completed messages as agentMessage instead of leaving them non-agent/unknown, so TypeScript consumers inherit a wrong Codex classification contract.
wrapper/src/main.rs writes tracing logs to stderr, contaminating the child stderr passthrough stream and breaking byte-for-byte proxy transparency whenever wrapper diagnostics are emitted.
wrapper/src/proxy.rs aborts stdout/stderr draining after a hardcoded 3 second timeout once the child exits, which can drop trailing proxied output under backpressure.

wrapper/src/ws_client.rs preserves attached=true across relay disconnects, so the wrapper can queue an unbounded backlog of forwarded relay output while no socket is connected and later replay stale messages after reconnect.

server/src/session-registry.ts allows idle sessions to transition directly to waitingApproval on any serverRequest frame, which violates the required executing -> waitingApproval state machine and can incorrectly increment turnCount on a later turn/completed.
server/src/relay-server.ts only replays attach-status-changed when attachedUser is truthy, so reconnecting wrappers can retain stale attached state after an offline detach.
server/src/relay-server.ts encodes approval replies as {type:"input",approval:boolean}, but wrapper/src/ws_client.rs ignores that wire format, so approval responses never reach codex.

appliedUpdates.description: Added a reusable lint-wrapper command that extracts Debian's rust-clippy package into a temporary PATH and runs cargo clippy against the wrapper crate.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Replace the unconditional `cargo clippy` step with guidance to use `.factory/services.yaml`'s `lint-wrapper` command or the documented rust-clippy extraction workaround when clippy is not on PATH.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: wrapper-stdio-proxy, wrapper-config, and wrapper-ws-client reviews all found workers rediscovering the same Debian environment limitation even though `.factory/library/environment.md` already documents it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document an exception for scaffold/setup tasks whose feature contract explicitly allows zero tests so workers do not have to violate the skill's mandatory RED-test-first wording.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The project-scaffold review found the feature correctly delivered setup work with no tests while still marking the current ts-worker procedure as followed, showing the skill text does not fit this task class.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the current TypeScript workspace import expectations for the `shared` package (or required build/reference setup) before instructing workers to always import shared types directly from `shared`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The server-session-registry review found the existing guidance to import from `shared` conflicts with the current package exports/tsconfig wiring, which led the worker to probe module resolution and duplicate core types locally.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a protocol-compatibility rule that classification tables, attachment reconnect semantics, and approval-response wire formats must be updated consistently across Rust, shared TypeScript, and mission contracts in the same change.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews found mismatches in shared vs Rust message classification (project-scaffold and wrapper-message-classifier), reconnect attachment expectations (wrapper-ws-client and server-message-relay), and approval reply payload format (server-message-relay).

rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the broader protocol-compatibility guidance recommendation above.

rejectedObservations.observation: features.json and validation-contract.md disagree on whether missing relay configuration should fail fast or degrade gracefully.
rejectedObservations.reason: out-of-scope mission-contract inconsistency; it does not fit the AGENTS/skill guidance bucket and was not safe to rewrite during scrutiny.

rejectedObservations.observation: Library documentation omits explicit attachment-state behavior across relay reconnects.
rejectedObservations.reason: documentation-only library update deferred under the current no-doc-edit task constraints.
